step_by_step_reasoning: We identified the internal developer trigger, API Gateway, two separate Step Functions instances (AVM and Blueprint) matching the whiteboard, DynamoDB state storage, Control Tower account creation, the target AWS account, and SES email notifications. The workflow sequences these components exactly as drawn on the whiteboard.

graph.name: Vitesco Technologies Automated Account Vending Machine
graph.notes: End-to-end automated cloud landing zone using Control Tower and Step Functions to provision and configure AWS accounts across stages.

nodes.name: Internal User
nodes.notes: Requests new AWS account via portal.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Exposes API endpoint for the web portal requests.

nodes.name: Step Functions (AVM)
nodes.notes: Account Vending Machine to coordinate account provisioning workflows.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores target state config, ensuring process idempotency.

nodes.name: Control Tower
nodes.notes: Creates new AWS accounts using Service Catalog items.

nodes.name: Step Functions (Blueprint)
nodes.notes: Applies standard blueprints (IAM, networking, security) into target accounts.

nodes.name: AWS Account
nodes.notes: The newly provisioned, configured target tenant account.

nodes.name: SES
nodes.notes: Sends email notification to the user upon successful account setup.

edges.notes: Submits metadata for network setup and blueprints.
Applies network, IAM, and security configurations to target account.
Notifies user of account credentials and owner responsibilities.
